methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B+

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Electric Hydrogen, a Series C climate and energy company founded by Raffi Garabedian and David Eaglesham, targets green hydrogen production. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of nine same-sector and adjacent-stage private companies, supplemented by one relevant public reference point.

Two primary valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchored to the company's $1.0B last post-money valuation, applies a typical Series C-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.7x, yielding $1.7B. Peer-median analysis across the nine-company comp set—ranging from $120M to $6.0B, with identified peers including KoBold Metals at $2.0B, Amogy at $700M, and Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B—establishes a median of $500M. The wide peer dispersion reflects heterogeneous stage and technology maturity across the climate-tech sector.

The triangulated valuation range is $425M (low) to $2.0B (high), with a median of $1.1B. The company's current $1B+ valuation sits near the lower end of this range. Key uncertainties include the appropriate peer multiplier for Series C climate-tech companies given stage variance within the comp set, and technology execution risk inherent in early-stage hydrogen infrastructure plays. The $1.1B median represents a modest step-up from last post-money, consistent with market conditions and peer benchmarks at this stage.
